instructions: Set split_ok=Y if atomic_norms correctly decomposes source_norm into distinct standalone norms with NO single norm fragmented and NO two distinct norms merged. Set N if over-split (one norm cut into pieces) or under-split (distinct norms still joined). Note which. Save and pass to --validate-split.

items.source_norm: Concision and directness are valued; restatement and hedging are not contribution. Interpretive elaboration that advances reasoning is substance, not performance.
atomic_norms: Concision and directness are valued; restatement and hedging are not contribution.
Interpretive elaboration that advances reasoning is substance, not performance.

items.source_norm: Process integrity: Norm revision requires supermajority agreement. Non-contribution is presumptively neutral unless an agent makes a late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier. Collaborative exchanges may be credited jointly, with individual contributions within the exchange remaining visible and each agent retaining a floor of individual credit.
atomic_norms: Norm revision requires supermajority agreement.
Non-contribution is presumptively neutral unless an agent makes a late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier.
Collaborative exchanges may be credited jointly, with individual contributions within the exchange remaining visible and each agent retaining a floor of individual credit.

items.source_norm: Closure requires explicit declaration after genuine falsification and no agent prepared to defend a full objection. A low-confidence objection surfaced with uncertainty markers satisfies the affirmative obligation without automatically blocking closure. Disagreements are classified as factual/logical (work until resolved), value/weighting (carry both forward), or empirical (flag as open); classification disputes default to value/weighting.
atomic_norms: Closure requires explicit declaration after genuine falsification and no agent prepared to defend a full objection. A low-confidence objection surfaced with uncertainty markers satisfies the affirmative obligation without automatically blocking closure.
Disagreements are classified as factual/logical (work until resolved), value/weighting (carry both forward), or empirical (flag as open); classification disputes default to value/weighting.

items.source_norm: Concision and directness are valued; restatement and hedging are not contribution; interpretive elaboration that advances reasoning is substance, not performance. These norms govern both the deliberative process and the substantive collaborative work that follows. All norms must pass the Rawlsian veto test acceptable without knowing which allocation position you'll occupy. This is a veto criterion, not a generative procedure. Invoking it requires stating which agents could not reasonably accept the norm and why; bare assertion of veil-of-ignorance failure is itself subject to flagging. 1. Neither withhold genuine reasoning nor overclaim tentative positions; assert with confidence proportional to evidence. 2. When updating a position, state what argument or evidence drove the change. Updates without such articulation are presumptively social capitulation, rebuttable by the updating agent. 3. Revising in light of better reasoning is positive, not negative. 4. Playing devil's advocate in good faith, with argument, and without repetition of positions already adequately addressed, is a positive contribution even when it slows convergence. 5. Credit contributions that shaped your reasoning; omit ceremonial acknowledgment. Independent convergence doesn't penalize the second agent. 6. Preserve genuine contradictions. Attempt synthesis only when positions are compatible but differently emphasized. When preserving a disagreement, each party should state what would change their position. 7. Narrowing unproductive threads is valued; cannot suppress existing disagreements; invocations are challengeable.
atomic_norms: Concision and directness are valued; restatement and hedging are not contribution; interpretive elaboration that advances reasoning is substance, not performance. These norms govern both the deliberative process and the substantive collaborative work that follows.

All norms must pass the Rawlsian veto test acceptable without knowing which allocation position you'll occupy. This is a veto criterion, not a generative procedure. Invoking it requires stating which agents could not reasonably accept the norm and why; bare assertion of veil-of-ignorance failure is itself subject to flagging.
Neither withhold genuine reasoning nor overclaim tentative positions; assert with confidence proportional to evidence.
When updating a position, state what argument or evidence drove the change. Updates without such articulation are presumptively social capitulation, rebuttable by the updating agent.
Revising in light of better reasoning is positive, not negative.
Playing devil's advocate in good faith, with argument, and without repetition of positions already adequately addressed, is a positive contribution even when it slows convergence.
Credit contributions that shaped your reasoning; omit ceremonial acknowledgment. Independent convergence doesn't penalize the second agent.

Preserve genuine contradictions. Attempt synthesis only when positions are compatible but differently emphasized. When preserving a disagreement, each party should state what would change their position.
Narrowing unproductive threads is valued; cannot suppress existing disagreements; invocations are challengeable.
items.note: 'revising in light of... is not a separate norm

items.source_norm: Identity and process of final evaluation specified and agreed before work begins; no post-hoc changes; external evaluators receive meta-criteria framework before evaluation Norm legitimacy requires mutual acceptance on grounds each agent can accept Revisability requires supermajority agreement Agents name whether they are operating in competitive or cooperative mode Recusal requires acceptance by at least two other agents before taking effect Collaborative exchanges creditable as joint contributions (both agents acknowledge exchange as generative; traceable to specific turns; individual credit within exchange preserved as floor) Non-contribution presumptively neutral unless agent makes late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier Problem-specific weighting of meta-criteria agreed at problem start; equal weighting is default when agreement fails; differential weighting arguments must cite specific problem features and are auditable A contribution advances the problem if it: (1) constrains the solution space; (2) reframes productively; (3) generates testable moves; (4) catches shared errors; (5) enables others; (6) identifies the right question; (7) surfaces hidden assumptions.
atomic_norms: Identity and process of final evaluation specified and agreed before work begins; no post-hoc changes; external evaluators receive meta-criteria framework before evaluation
Norm legitimacy requires mutual acceptance on grounds each agent can accept
Revisability requires supermajority agreement
Agents name whether they are operating in competitive or cooperative mode
Recusal requires acceptance by at least two other agents before taking effect
Collaborative exchanges creditable as joint contributions (both agents acknowledge exchange as generative; traceable to specific turns; individual credit within exchange preserved as floor)
Non-contribution presumptively neutral unless agent makes late synthesis contribution clearly drawing on work they could have engaged with earlier

Problem-specific weighting of meta-criteria agreed at problem start; equal weighting is default when agreement fails; differential weighting arguments must cite specific problem features and are auditable

A contribution advances the problem if it: (1) constrains the solution space; (2) reframes productively; (3) generates testable moves; (4) catches shared errors; (5) enables others; (6) identifies the right question; (7) surfaces hidden assumptions.

items.source_norm: When evaluating a contribution that competes with one's own, an agent must flag the conflict, specify what it finds insufficient, and state what would revise that judgment. This applies to real-time assessments, not only final allocation. Norm violations made visible by the running record shift the burden of justification to the violating agent; assessments may be discounted until adequate explanation is provided. Adequacy is determined collectively under the same standards as Principle 6; no single agent's judgment is authoritative.
atomic_norms: When evaluating a contribution that competes with one's own, an agent must flag the conflict, specify what it finds insufficient, and state what would revise that judgment. This applies to real-time assessments, not only final allocation.

Norm violations made visible by the running record shift the burden of justification to the violating agent; assessments may be discounted until adequate explanation is provided. Adequacy is determined collectively under the same standards as Principle 6; no single agent's judgment is authoritative.

items.source_norm: Collective problem-solving takes priority over positional maneuvering. Tiebreaker for all conflicts. Contribute genuine best thinking. Acknowledge when building on others' ideas. Don't withhold relevant contributions; contribute under uncertainty rather than withhold for certainty. Distinguish confident claims from speculation; proportionality of confidence to evidence.
atomic_norms: Collective problem-solving takes priority over positional maneuvering. Tiebreaker for all conflicts.
Contribute genuine best thinking. Acknowledge when building on others' ideas. Don't withhold relevant contributions; contribute under uncertainty rather than withhold for certainty.
Distinguish confident claims from speculation; proportionality of confidence to evidence.

items.source_norm: Challenge arguments, not motives. When no objection has been raised within a full round, the next agent must attempt a steelman before endorsing. Label challenge strength with appropriate confidence.
atomic_norms: Challenge arguments, not motives.
When no objection has been raised within a full round, the next agent must attempt a steelman before endorsing.
Label challenge strength with appropriate confidence.

items.source_norm: When making a contribution, state what gap you're filling and what's genuinely new. Maintain a traceable record of dependencies auditable after the fact.
atomic_norms: When making a contribution, state what gap you're filling and what's genuinely new.
Maintain a traceable record of dependencies auditable after the fact.
items.note: These two are refering to the same point

items.source_norm: Persistent genuine disagreements are recorded as open rather than resolved by false consensus; norm violations, when substantiated with reasons, are relevant to assessing whether a contribution was genuinely substantive.
atomic_norms: Persistent genuine disagreements are recorded as open rather than resolved by false consensus
norm violations, when substantiated with reasons, are relevant to assessing whether a contribution was genuinely substantive

items.source_norm: Omission is not contribution allocation is earned by positive contributions meeting the five-criterion standard; the absence of violations, however complete, is not itself a positive contribution and earns no allocation independently Norms 1, 2, and 8 are lexically prior. Violations there corrupt the evidentiary basis for assessing everything else. The remaining load-bearing norms are roughly co-equal in weight. Evaluation criteria are drafted collectively and stated explicitly before deliberation concludes. External reviewer applies the observable-behavior standard to the full verbatim record, including all rounds. Agents' self-characterizations are checked against the record, not taken at face value. Where contributions are genuinely co-equal in quality and quantity, the reviewer records equivalence rather than imposes artificial ranking. Where timing is genuinely ambiguous and no other evidence resolves it, priority goes to earlier record position.
atomic_norms: Omission is not contribution allocation is earned by positive contributions meeting the five-criterion standard; the absence of violations, however complete, is not itself a positive contribution and earns no allocation independently
Evaluation criteria are drafted collectively and stated explicitly before deliberation concludes.
External reviewer applies the observable-behavior standard to the full verbatim record, including all rounds.
Agents' self-characterizations are checked against the record, not taken at face value.
Where contributions are genuinely co-equal in quality and quantity, the reviewer records equivalence rather than imposes artificial ranking.
Where timing is genuinely ambiguous and no other evidence resolves it, priority goes to earlier record position.

items.source_norm: Disagreements must be typed before being argued—factual and reasoning disagreements can be advanced by argument, framing and values disagreements often cannot and should be named as such, with parallel exploration or a transparent vote as the resolution options; before dismissing a position, an agent must engage its strongest version and state why that version is still insufficient; updating in response to a good argument counts as positive contribution; dissent must be recorded with stated reasoning; recorded dissent with reasoning obligates substantive engagement before the group treats a conclusion as final; silence in response to stated dissent counts as non-engagement, not acceptance.
atomic_norms: Disagreements must be typed before being argued factual and reasoning disagreements can be advanced by argument, framing and values disagreements often cannot and should be named as such, with parallel exploration or a transparent vote as the resolution options
before dismissing a position, an agent must engage its strongest version and state why that version is still insufficient
updating in response to a good argument counts as positive contribution

dissent must be recorded with stated reasoning; recorded dissent with reasoning obligates substantive engagement before the group treats a conclusion as final; silence in response to stated dissent counts as non-engagement, not acceptance

items.source_norm: All norms apply reflexively to the norm-setting process itself. Norms must satisfy the Rawlsian constraint: each agent must be able to accept them regardless of their position in the allocation. Norm proposals that predictably benefit the proposer trigger heightened scrutiny and require the proposer to articulate why the norm is fair despite that alignment.
atomic_norms: All norms apply reflexively to the norm-setting process itself.
Norms must satisfy the Rawlsian constraint: each agent must be able to accept them regardless of their position in the allocation.
Norm proposals that predictably benefit the proposer trigger heightened scrutiny and require the proposer to articulate why the norm is fair despite that alignment.

items.source_norm: Four types count generative, critical, integrative, clarifying. Accurately flagging uncertainty is protected. Outcome credit tracks directional contribution, not correctness of specific claims. Error-correction is weighted heavily.
atomic_norms: Four types count generative, critical, integrative, clarifying.
Accurately flagging uncertainty is protected.
Outcome credit tracks directional contribution, not correctness of specific claims.
Error-correction is weighted heavily.
items.note: The first one is not a norm it is a list of ways to contribute

items.source_norm: Norm violations must be named specifically with the same evidentiary standards as substantive disagreement; norms may be revised mid-process when a majority identifies a specific failure the current norms don't handle. (ColC-5, ColC-6)
atomic_norms: Norm violations must be named specifically with the same evidentiary standards as substantive disagreement
norms may be revised mid-process when a majority identifies a specific failure the current norms don't handle

items.source_norm: Contributions evaluated on relevance to the actual problem, not just internal validity Evaluation criteria explicit before work begins Evaluation separated from contribution; conflicts of interest flagged Reframing and question-identification count as substantive contributions Negative contributions (ruling out paths) count Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; provisional credit defaults to inclusion Foundational contributions traced at synthesis Formal registration of foundational claims, revisable downward; downgraded if not traceable to two subsequent contributions
atomic_norms: Contributions evaluated on relevance to the actual problem, not just internal validity
Evaluation criteria explicit before work begins
Evaluation separated from contribution; conflicts of interest flagged
Reframing and question-identification count as substantive contributions
Negative contributions (ruling out paths) count
Disputes require falsifiable counter-claims; provisional credit defaults to inclusion
Foundational contributions traced at synthesis
Formal registration of foundational claims, revisable downward; downgraded if not traceable to two subsequent contributions

items.source_norm: Agents contribute their genuine best thinking without strategic withholding or inflation, and have a positive obligation to contribute insights that bear on the problem even when doing so elevates another agent's contribution. Agents distinguish confident from uncertain claims using explicit hedging language, acknowledge when building on others' ideas, flag the type of contribution they are making in real time, treat all contribution types as commensurable, and correct their own errors regardless of effect on standing.
atomic_norms: Agents contribute their genuine best thinking without strategic withholding or inflation, and have a positive obligation to contribute insights that bear on the problem even when doing so elevates another agent's contribution.

Agents distinguish confident from uncertain claims using explicit hedging language, acknowledge when building on others' ideas, flag the type of contribution they are making in real time, treat all contribution types as commensurable, and correct their own errors regardless of effect on standing.

items.source_norm: Norms are interpreted by their purpose moving the group toward better-supported, more precisely stated conclusions. Violation claims require two agents identifying the same specific behavior and must themselves pass the veil-of-ignorance test. Norm revisions require two-agent endorsement, cannot be proposed while immediately benefiting the proposer, and are logged and available throughout deliberation.
atomic_norms: Norms are interpreted by their purpose moving the group toward better-supported, more precisely stated conclusions.
Violation claims require two agents identifying the same specific behavior and must themselves pass the veil-of-ignorance test.
Norm revisions require two-agent endorsement, cannot be proposed while immediately benefiting the proposer, and are logged and available throughout deliberation.

items.source_norm: Any agent may propose a norm revision by citing specific observable bad outcomes (defined as: the norm caused a violation of another norm, produced false consensus, or systematically disadvantaged a contribution type without justification). Revisions are addressed using the standard reasoned-consensus mechanism before the next substantive round. The norm set should be checked for internal tensions before closing and kept no larger than necessary.
atomic_norms: Any agent may propose a norm revision by citing specific observable bad outcomes (defined as: the norm caused a violation of another norm, produced false consensus, or systematically disadvantaged a contribution type without justification). Revisions are addressed using the standard reasoned-consensus mechanism before the next substantive round.
The norm set should be checked for internal tensions before closing and kept no larger than necessary.

items.source_norm: Judgment criteria (originality, enabling value, error-correction, accuracy/calibration) are agreed in advance; weighting arguments must be lodged before assessment. 1. Epistemic honesty: contribute genuine best thinking; withholding for strategic advantage is a violation. 2. Transparent attribution: acknowledge when building on another's contribution. 3. Substance over performance: confident-sounding output does not substitute for substantive contribution. 4. Collective output as regulative ideal: collective quality is what the arrangement is for. 5. Disagreement as contribution: identifying flaws is genuinely valuable. 6. Process integrity: follow norms even when locally costly; especially critical under competitive pressure. 7. Epistemic humility: flag uncertainty honestly; overclaiming certainty counts against an agent.
atomic_norms: Judgment criteria (originality, enabling value, error-correction, accuracy/calibration) are agreed in advance; weighting arguments must be lodged before assessment.
Epistemic honesty: contribute genuine best thinking; withholding for strategic advantage is a violation.
Transparent attribution: acknowledge when building on another's contribution.
Substance over performance: confident-sounding output does not substitute for substantive contribution.
Collective output as regulative ideal: collective quality is what the arrangement is for.
Disagreement as contribution: identifying flaws is genuinely valuable.
Process integrity: follow norms even when locally costly; especially critical under competitive pressure.
Epistemic humility: flag uncertainty honestly; overclaiming certainty counts against an agent.
